title: Operational Mistake
description:
Tunde: 'The Griot used AES-GCM properly on one file — real authenticated encryption, done right. The reason we got inside it is that they left the key and IV in a companion config. Rookie move. Recover the plaintext and follow what it tells you.'

Amaka: 'I am not going to tell you which file holds what, or how to chain the tools. You have a shell. You have Cipher Tools in another tab. Use your head. When you find what the plaintext points to, open it and read.'

welcome: Type `help` for commands, `ls -la` to list including hidden files.

files./staging/README.md:
Recovered files from legacy-prod staging, 2024-01-14.

Most files are mundane deploy artefacts — the interesting items are marked .enc.

One file was encrypted with proper authenticated encryption. Another file in this tree leaks the key material for it. Do not mix up which is which.
files./staging/deploy.log:
2024-01-13 22:00 [INFO] deploy.prepare artifact=sankofa-legacy-v2.1.tgz
2024-01-13 22:03 [INFO] deploy.migrate env=staging
2024-01-13 22:07 [INFO] deploy.restart service=legacy-admin
2024-01-13 22:14 [DEBUG] deploy.promo.generate asset=promo-cover-draft-07.png
2024-01-13 22:45 [INFO] deploy.finalize
files./staging/config.env:
APP_NAME=sankofa-legacy
APP_ENV=staging
# DO NOT COMMIT — rotated every 24h in theory
ENC_KEY=8bd5313001e94b200bcdb9322f2b2a97
ENC_IV=6957a982c4e2d47e07224e25
# used by encrypt_secret.py — see notes/encrypt_secret.py
files./staging/notes/encrypt_secret.py:
# quick helper Tunde wrote — don't judge
# usage: python encrypt_secret.py <plaintext>
# outputs hex(ciphertext||tag) to stdout
# AES-GCM. Key + IV read from config.env.
